| First game         = {{fb|LUX}} 1–4 {{fb-rt|FRA|1794}}<br/>([[Luxembourg City|Luxembourg]], [[Luxembourg]]; 29 October 1911)
| Largest win        = {{fb|LUX}} 6–0 {{fb-rt|AFG|1930}}<br/>([[Brighton]], [[England]]; 26 July 1948)
| Largest loss       = {{fb|GER|Nazi}} [[Football at the 1936 Summer Olympics#First round|9–0]] {{fb-rt|LUX}}<br/>([[Berlin]], [[Germany]]; 4 August 1936)<br/>{{fb|LUX}} 0–9 {{fb-rt|ENG}}<br/>([[Luxembourg City|Luxembourg]], [[Luxembourg]]; 19 October 1960)<br/>{{fb|ENG}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|LUX}}<br/>([[London]], [[England]]; 15 December 1982)<br/>{{fb|POR}} 9–0 {{fb-rt|LUX}}<br/>([[Almancil]], [[Portugal]]; 11 September 2023)
}}
The '''Luxembourg national football team''' (nicknamed the ''Red Lions''; {{langx|lb|Lëtzebuergesch Foussballnationalekipp}}, {{langx|fr|Équipe du Luxembourg de football}}, {{langx|de|Luxemburgische Fußballnationalmannschaft}}) is the national [[association football|football]] team of [[Luxembourg]], and is controlled by the [[Luxembourg Football Federation]]. The team plays most of its home matches at the [[Stade de Luxembourg]] in [[Luxembourg City]].

Luxembourg has participated in every [[FIFA World Cup]] qualifiers since those for the 1934 World Cup, and in [[UEFA European Championship]] qualifiers since those for Euro 1964. As of 2026, they have never qualified for any of these major tournaments. Luxembourg is the nation with the most qualifying campaigns in both of these competitions without ever making it to the finals. However, they did compete in six [[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic football events]] between 1920 and 1952.<ref name="results" />

==History==
{{multiple image
|align = left
|direction = vertical
|image1 = Luxembourg national football team 1920 year.jpg
|width1 = 250
|alt1 = 
|image2 = Luxembourg - Ukraine 2015 (1).jpg
|width2 = 250
|alt2 = 
|footer = The Luxembourg national football team in 1920 (above), and in 2015
}}
Luxembourg played their first ever international match on 29 October 1911, in a friendly match against [[France national football team|France]]; it resulted in a 1–4 defeat.<ref name = "results">{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesl/lux-intres.html|title=Luxembourg&nbsp;– List of International Matches|author=Barrie Courney|date=4 December 2014|access-date=31 August 2012|website=RSSSF|archive-date=6 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230406010422/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesl/lux-intres.html|url-status=live}}</ref> Their first victory came on 8 February 1914, also in a match against France, which they won 5–4.<ref name = "results"/>

The national side of Luxembourg competed in six [[Football at the Summer Olympics|Olympic football events]] between 1920 and 1952, and survived the preliminary round twice (in 1948 and 1952).<ref name = "results"/> In between, Luxembourg participated in qualifiers for the [[FIFA World Cup]], but has never qualified.

Starting in 1921, the Luxembourg national A-selection would play 239 unofficial international matches until 1981, mostly against other countries' B-teams like those of [[Belgium national football B team|Belgium]], [[France national football B team|France]], Switzerland and [[Germany national football B team|West Germany]], as well as a team representing South-Netherlands.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesl/lux-unoff-intres.html|first=Barrie|last=Courtney|title=Luxembourg {{!}} List of Unofficial International matches |website=[[RSSSF]]|date=8 March 2005 |access-date=12 September 2015 |archive-date=13 June 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230613234557/http://www.rsssf.org/tablesl/lux-unoff-intres.html |url-status=live }}</ref>

[[File:Voorronde van Wereldkampioenschap voetbal te Rotterdam, Nederland tegen Luxembur, Bestanddeelnr 922-2390.jpg|thumb|The Luxembourg team in 1969, before a World Cup qualifier]]
After their last Olympic tournament in 1952, the national team also started playing in qualifying groups for [[UEFA European Championship]]s, but could not reach the major European tournament end stages. The only time that the team was close to qualify was for a European or World Championship was for the [[Euro 1964]]. In the first qualification round, they defeated the [[Netherlands national football team|Netherlands]] with a score of 3–2 on aggregate after two matches. A Dutch newspaper commented this stunt after the second match with "David Luxembourg won with 2–1 [against [[Goliath]] Netherlands]".<ref>{{cite news |url=http://www.delpher.nl/nl/kranten/view?query=Luxemburg+voetbal&coll=ddd&page=5&facets%5Bperiode%5D%5B%5D=2%7C20e_eeuw%7C1960-1969%7C1963%7C&sortfield=datedesc&identifier=ddd%3A010679166%3Ampeg21%3Aa0230&resultsidentifier=ddd%3A010679166%3Ampeg21%3Aa0230 |title=Schwartz' droombeeld werd nachtmerrie voor publiek |newspaper=Nieuwsblad van het Noorden |language=nl |date=31 October 1963 |access-date=23 August 2015 |archive-date=23 September 2015 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150923225433/http://www.delpher.nl/nl/kranten/view?query=Luxemburg+voetbal&coll=ddd&page=5&facets%5Bperiode%5D%5B%5D=2%7C20e_eeuw%7C1960-1969%7C1963%7C&sortfield=datedesc&identifier=ddd%3A010679166%3Ampeg21%3Aa0230&resultsidentifier=ddd%3A010679166%3Ampeg21%3Aa0230 |url-status=live }}</ref> In the round of eight, Luxembourg and [[Denmark national football team|Denmark]] fought for a spot in the final tournament. The winner was decided after three matches; Denmark was the winner with a total aggregate score of 6–5.

When the national team does win a competitive match, they are often celebrated by national media and fans, as was the case after a 2–1 win against [[Switzerland national football team|Switzerland]] in 2008.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://tele.rtl.lu/waatleeft/replay/v/20110907/0/46473/|title=De Journal|website=[[RTL Lëtzebuerg]]|publisher=[[RTL Group]]|date=7 September 2008|access-date=25 March 2013|archive-date=29 July 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130729192018/http://tele.rtl.lu/waatleeft/replay/v/20110907/0/46473/|url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Luxembourg stuns Switzerland 2–1 in qualifying|url=https://www.foxsports.com/stories/soccer/luxembourg-stuns-switzerland-2-1-in-qualifying|website=[[Fox Sports]]|date=10 September 2008|access-date=26 April 2024}}</ref>

On 3 September 2017, Luxembourg faced France in a goalless draw at [[Stadium Municipal]] in [[Toulouse]], France.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/football/news/france-portugal-live-score-stream-11105831|title=World Cup qualifying recap as France are held by Luxembourg and Belgium qualify for Russia 2018|newspaper=[[Daily Mirror]]|publisher=[[Future plc]]|date=3 September 2017|access-date=3 September 2017|archive-date=3 September 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170903215433/http://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/football/news/france-portugal-live-score-stream-11105831|url-status=live}}</ref> It was the first time France had failed to win against Luxembourg since 1914, when Luxembourg won, 5–4.<ref>{{Cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesl/lux-intres.html |title=Luxembourg vs. France 5–4|website=RSSSF|date=8 February 1914|access-date=2 February 2023|archive-date=6 April 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230406010422/https://www.rsssf.org/tablesl/lux-intres.html |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/41138056|title=France coach Didier Deschamps was left "infuriated" by his side's failures in front of goal in their goalless draw with Luxembourg, a result labelled "historic" by his opposite number.|website=[[BBC Sport]]|date=4 September 2017|access-date=4 September 2017|archive-date=3 September 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170903220631/http://www.bbc.com/sport/football/41138056|url-status=live}}</ref> On 10 November 2017, Luxembourg defeated [[Hungary national football team|Hungary]] 2–1 in a friendly victory.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.chicago-fire.com/post/2017/11/09/nikolic-scores-hungary-falls-2-1-luxembourg-international-friendly|title=Nikolic scores as Hungary falls 2-1 to Luxembourg in international friendly &#124; Chicago Fire FC|access-date=2017-11-09|archive-date=2017-11-10|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20171110024245/https://www.chicago-fire.com/post/2017/11/09/nikolic-scores-hungary-falls-2-1-luxembourg-international-friendly|url-status=live}}</ref>

On 28 March 2021, Luxembourg beat the [[Republic of Ireland national football team|Republic of Ireland]] in a [[2022 FIFA World Cup qualification]] match with a goal from [[Gerson Rodrigues (footballer, born 1995)|Gerson Rodrigues]] in the 85th minute.<ref>{{cite web |last=Cooney |first=Gavin |date=27 March 2021 |title=Awful Ireland embarrassed in defeat at home to Luxembourg |url=https://www.the42.ie/ireland-luxembourg-2-5393924-Mar2021/ |access-date=31 March 2021|website=[[TheJournal.ie]]|archive-date=10 April 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230410013441/https://www.the42.ie/ireland-luxembourg-2-5393924-Mar2021/ |url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=Republic defeat 'embarrassing', Coleman |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/56552884 |access-date=11 January 2014|website=BBC Sport|archive-date=30 March 2021|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210330174611/https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/56552884 |url-status=live }}</ref> In 2023, Luxembourg achieved some of their best results in European Championship qualifying, finishing [[UEFA Euro 2024 qualifying Group J|third in their group]] (of six teams) and qualifying for a play-off for a finals berth,<ref>{{Cite web |last=Horváth |first=Bence |date=19 October 2023 |title=Football in Luxembourg: Euro 2024: Playoffs provide lifeline for the Red Lions |url=https://today.rtl.lu/sport/local/a/2128132.html |url-status=live|website=RTL Lëtzebuerg|access-date=11 January 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111183742/https://today.rtl.lu/sport/local/a/2128132.html |archive-date=11 January 2024}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Mackey |first=Ed |title=Euro 2024 play-offs: All you need to know |url=https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/5068159/2023/11/23/euro-2024-play-offs-teams-dates/ |access-date=11 January 2024|newspaper=[[The Athletic]]|date=4 December 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111183741/https://theathletic.com/5068159/2023/11/23/euro-2024-play-offs-teams-dates/|archive-date=11 January 2024|url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |title=Luxembourg 4-1 Bosnia and Herzegovina|url=https://www.espn.com/soccer/report/_/gameId/655438|publisher=[[ESPN]]|date=16 November 2023|access-date=26 April 2024}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |first=Bence|last=Horváth|date=22 November 2023 |title=International football: Luxembourg to play Georgia at Euro 2024 playoff round |url=https://today.rtl.lu/sport/local/a/2140702.html|website=RTL Lëtzebuerg|access-date=11 January 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111183743/https://today.rtl.lu/sport/local/a/2140702.html|archive-date=11 January 2024|url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Luxembourg football scales new heights |url=https://www.uefa.com/news-media/news/0288-199762623e72-2493528ebf56-1000--luxembourg-football-scales-new-heights/|website=[[UEFA]]|date=1 December 2023|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240110171045/https://www.uefa.com/insideuefa/news/0288-199762623e72-2493528ebf56-1000--luxembourg-football-scales-new-heights/|archive-date=10 January 2024|url-status=live }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |date=11 September 2023|title=The development of football in Luxembourg|url=https://www.365scores.com/news/the-development-of-football-in-luxembourg|website=365scores|access-date=11 January 2024|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240111191251/https://www.365scores.com/news/the-development-of-football-in-luxembourg|archive-date=11 January 2024|url-status=live}}</ref> but they lost 2–0 against [[Georgia national football team|Georgia]].<ref>{{Cite web|first=Gaël|last=Arellano|title=Euro 2024 Qualifiers: VAR heartbreak for Luxembourg in Georgia|url=https://today.rtl.lu/sport/local/a/2180029.html|website=RTL Lëtzebuerg|date=21 March 2024|access-date=26 April 2024}}</ref> Rodrigues also became the top goalscorer in the history of the national team, surpassing the 16-goal tally by [[Léon Mart]], by scoring five goals during the qualifying process, a record for a Luxembourgian footballer.<ref>{{Cite web|last=Inshakov|first=Maksym |date=14 October 2023 |title=Dynamo footballer scores a goal in 56 seconds, setting a record. Video |url=https://eng.obozrevatel.com/section-news/news-dynamo-footballer-scores-a-goal-in-56-seconds-setting-a-record-video-14-10-2023.html |website=Obozrevatel|access-date=19 February 2024}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Dunbar |first=Graham |date=20 March 2024|title=Luxembourg looks to be soccer's next Cinderella story going into Euro 2024 playoffs |url=https://apnews.com/article/euro-2024-playoffs-luxembourg-philipp-b7b39d6f26ccda7ee3f94916e2f3520a|work=[[Associated Press News]]|access-date=26 April 2024}}</ref>

On 22 March 2025, Luxembourg showed improvement with a convincing victory against [[Sweden men's national football team|Sweden]] in a friendly match.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Friendlies Sweden vs. Luxembourg|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c14j203n034t|website=BBC Sport|date=22 March 2025|access-date=13 May 2025}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.chronicle.lu/category/football/54034-luxembourg-secure-first-ever-football-win-against-sweden|title=Luxembourg Secure First-Ever Football Win Against Sweden|website=chronicle.lu |date=24 March 2025|access-date=25 March 2025}}</ref>

| 2022–present
|-
|}

==Home stadium==
[[File:Luxembourg, stade de Luxembourg (101).jpg|thumb|right|Stade de Luxembourg]]
As of 1 September 2021, the Luxembourg national team adopted Luxembourg City's [[Stade de Luxembourg]], the country's [[national stadium]], as its home venue. Formerly, the team played at the [[Stade Josy Barthel]], where, at counting in August 2015, it had played 235 games, including unofficial matches.<ref>{{cite web|title=Stade Josy Barthel, Lëtzebuerg|url=http://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=447&page=7 |website=eu-football.info|access-date=23 Aug 2015 |archive-date=7 September 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230907184603/https://eu-football.info/_venue.php?id=447&page=7 |url-status=live }}</ref>
=== Supporters ===

The [[M-Block Fanatics]] is a non-profit football supporters' association for the Luxembourg national football team. M-Block Fanatics was founded in 1995 and derives its name from the fact that originally they used to occupy the ''M-Block'' of the former National Stadium.

==Results and fixtures==

|stadium        = [[Stade de Luxembourg]]
|attendance     = 9,113
|referee        = [[Daniel Siebert (referee)|Daniel Siebert]] ([[German Football Association|Germany]])
|result         = W
}}

==Staff==
[[File:Luc Holtz.jpg|thumb|250px|Luc Holtz, the Luxembourg manager from 2010 to 2025]]
As of 2025, the staff of the Luxembourg national team includes the following members:<ref>{{cite web|title=Staff|url=https://www.flf.lu/kader-staff-80254v4/groups/142533/seasons/212|publisher=Luxembourg Football Federation|access-date=18 November 2022|language=fr|archive-date=18 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240218165812/https://www.flf.lu/kader-staff-80254v4/groups/142533/seasons/212 |url-status=live }}</ref>

{| class="wikitable"
|-
!Position
!Name
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|LUX}} [[Manuel Cardoni]]
|-
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|LUX}} [[Jeff Strasser]]
|-
|Assistant coach
|{{flagicon|LUX}} [[Mario Mutsch]]
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Rui Forte
|-
|Fitness coach
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Claude Origer
|-
|rowspan=2|Doctors
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Patrick Dang
|-
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Lara Heinz
|-
|rowspan=3|Physiotherapists
|{{flagicon|GER}} Alexander Kähler
|-
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Erwan Deshoux
|-
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Gilles Hoffmann
|-
|Video analyst
|{{flagicon|FRA}} Clément Gonin
|-
|rowspan=3|Equipment manager
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Léon Huss
|-
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Jos Koecher
|-
|{{flagicon|LUX}} Romain Sailer
|}

===Coaching history===
The following managers have been in charge of Luxembourg's national squad:<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.profootball.lu/Navigation+2/Entra%C3%AEneurs+nationaux.html|title=Les entraîneurs nationaux du Luxembourg|language=fr|access-date=31 August 2012|website=profootball.lu|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120625001705/http://www.profootball.lu/Navigation+2/Entra%C3%AEneurs+nationaux.html|archive-date=25 June 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref>

{{div col|colwidth=20em}}
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Paul Feierstein]] (1933–1948)
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Jean-Pierre Hoscheid]], [[Jules Müller]], & [[Albert Reuter]] (1948–1949)
*{{flagicon|Austria}} [[Adolf Patek]] (1949–1953)
*{{flagicon|Hungary|1949}} [[Béla Volentik]] (1953–1955)
*{{flagicon|Austria}} [[Eduard Havlicek]] (1955)
*{{flagicon|Hungary}} [[Nándor Lengyel]] (1955–1959)
*{{flagicon|France|1794}} [[Pierre Sinibaldi]] (1959–1960)
*{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[Robert Heinz]] (1960–1969)
*{{flagicon|Austria}} [[Ernst Melchior]] (1969–1972)
*{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[Gilbert Legrand]] (1972–1977)
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Arthur Schoos]] (1978)
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Louis Pilot]] (1978–1984)
*{{flagicon|Belgium}} [[Jozef Vliers]] (1984)
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Josy Kirchens]] (1985)
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Paul Philipp]] (1985–2001)
*{{flagicon|Denmark}} [[Allan Simonsen]] (2001–2004)
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Guy Hellers]] (2004–2010)
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Luc Holtz]] (2010–2025)
*{{flagicon|Luxembourg}} [[Jeff Strasser]] (2025–)
{{div col end}}

==Players==

===Current squad===
The following players were called up for the [[Exhibition game|friendly]] matches against [[Italy national football team|Italy]] and [[Albania national football team|Albania]] on 3 and 6 June 2026.<ref>{{cite web|title=LA SÉLECTION DES ROUT LÉIWEN POUR LES MATCHS CONTRE L'ITALIE ET L'ALBANIE|trans-title=The selection of Red Lions for matches against Italy and Albania|url=https://www.flf.lu/post-296485v4/posts/66232|publisher=Luxembourg Football Federation|date=21 May 2026|access-date=21 May 2026|language=fr}}</ref>

''Caps and goals as of 6 June 2026 after the match against [[Albania national football team|Albania]].''<ref>{{cite web |title=Luxembourg vs. Albania|url=https://www.soccerway.com/match/albania-ELP2K4oB/luxembourg-2cnFSaNj/|website=Soccerway|date=6 June 2026|access-date=6 June 2026}}</ref>

===Recent call-ups===

{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Yvandro Borges Sanches]]|sortname=Sanches, Yvandro Borges|age={{birth date and age|2004|5|24|df=y}}|caps=29|goals=3|club=[[Heracles Almelo]]|clubnat=NED|latest=v. {{fb|ITA}}, 3 June 2026}}<sup>INJ</sup>
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Jayson Videira]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|2|17|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[1. FSV Mainz 05 II|Mainz 05 II]]|clubnat=GER|latest=v. {{fb|MLT}}, 31 March 2026}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Kenan Avdusinovic]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|3|3|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[FC Progrès Niederkorn|Progrès Niederkorn]]|clubnat=LUX|latest=v. {{fb|NIR}}, 17 November 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Adulai Djabi Embalo]]|sortname=Embalo, Adulai Djabi|age={{birth date and age|2005|06|20|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Virtus Entella]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|NIR}}, 17 November 2025}}
{{nat fs break}}
;Notes
*<sup>PRE</sup> = Preliminary squad/standby.
*<sup>INJ</sup> = Not part of the [[#Current squad|current squad]] due to injury.
*<sup>WD</sup> = Player withdrew from the current squad due to non-injury issue.
*<sup>U21</sup> = Player joined under-21 team after opening game of window.
{{nat fs end}}

==Player records==
{{main|List of Luxembourg international footballers}}

{{updated|6 June 2026}}<ref name="RSSSF">{{cite web |last=Mamrud |first=Roberto |title=Luxembourg {{!}} Record International Players |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/lux-recintlp.html |website=RSSSF |access-date=8 February 2019 |archive-date=24 March 2023 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230324222306/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/lux-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
Players in '''bold''' are still active with Luxembourg.

===Most capped players===
[[File:RC Lens - FC Metz (09-02-2019) 57.jpg|175px|right|thumb|Laurent Jans is Luxembourg's most capped player with 124 appearances.]]

|98

|align=left|'''[[Christopher Martins]]'''

|-
|10
|align=left|[[Gilbert Dussier]]
|9
|39
|{{#expr:9/39 round 2}}
|1971–1978
|}

===Olympic Games===
[[File:BEL-LUX SO1928.JPG|thumb|Hectic phase during the goal-rich Olympic defeat against [[Belgium national football team|Belgium]] in 1928 (5–3)]]
